contentBlocks.original:
ॐ मौनव्याख्या प्रकटितपरब्रह्मतत्वंयुवानं
वर्शिष्ठान्तेवसदृषिगणैरावृतं ब्रह्मनिष्ठैः |
आचार्येन्द्रं करकलित चिन्मुद्रमानन्दमूर्तिं
स्वात्मरामं मुदितवदनं दक्षिणामूर्तिमीडे ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ maunavyākhyā prakaṭita parabrahmatatvaṃ yuvānaṃ
varśiṣṭhānte vasad ṛṣigaṇair āvṛtaṃ brahmaniṣṭhaiḥ |
ācāryendraṃ karakalita cinmudram ānandamūrtiṃ
svātmarāmaṃ muditavadanaṃ dakṣiṇāmūrtimīḍe ‖
contentBlocks.translation: I salute Sri Dakshinamurti, the Young Guru, who teaches the knowledge of Brahman through silence, who is surrounded by disciples, who are themselves ṛṣis and scholars in the Vedas. (I worship Śrī Dakṣiṇāmūrti), who is the teacher of teachers, whose hand is held in the sign of knowledge (cin-mudrā), whose nature is fullness, who reveals in himself, and who is ever silent. (1)
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; मौनव्याख्या (maunavyākhyā) – explanation through silence (mauna:silence | vyākhyā:explanation); प्रकटित (prakaṭita) – revealed; परब्रह्मतत्वं (parabrahmatattvaṃ) – essence of supreme Brahman (para:supreme | brahma:Brahman | tattvaṃ:essence); युवानं (yuvānaṃ) – youthful; वर्शिष्ठान्तेवसदृषिगणैः (varśiṣṭhāntevasadṛṣigaṇaiḥ) – surrounded by great sages (varśiṣṭha:great | antevasad:disciples | ṛṣigaṇaiḥ:by sages); आवृतं (āvṛtaṃ) – surrounded; ब्रह्मनिष्ठैः (brahmaniṣṭhaiḥ) – devoted to Brahman (brahma:Brahman | niṣṭhaiḥ:devoted); आचार्येन्द्रं (ācāryendraṃ) – teacher of teachers (ācārya:teacher | indraṃ:chief); करकलित (karakalita) – held in hand (kara:hand | kalita:held); चिन्मुद्रं (cinmudraṃ) – gesture of knowledge (cit:knowledge | mudraṃ:gesture); आनन्दमूर्तिं (ānandamūrtiṃ) – embodiment of bliss (ānanda:bliss | mūrtiṃ:embodiment); स्वात्मरामं (svātmarāmaṃ) – rejoicing in self (sva:self | ātmarāmaṃ:rejoicing); मुदितवदनं (muditavadanaṃ) – with joyful face (mudita:joyful | vadanaṃ:face); दक्षिणामूर्तिम् (dakṣiṇāmūrtim) – Dakshinamurti; ईडे (īḍe) – I worship;

contentBlocks.original:
वटविटपिसमीपे भूमिभागे निषण्णं
सकलमुनिजनानां ज्ञानदातारमारात् |
त्रिभुवनगुरुमीशं दक्षिणामूर्तिदेवं
जननमरणदुःखच्छेद दक्षं नमामि ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaṭaviṭapisamīpe bhūmibhāge niṣaṇṇaṃ
sakalamunijanānāṃ jñānadātāramārāt |
tribhuvana guru mīśaṃ dakṣiṇāmūrtidevaṃ
janana maraṇa duḥkha cchedadakṣaṃ namāmi ‖
contentBlocks.translation: I bow down to Sri Dakshinamurti, the teacher of the three worlds, the Lord, the destroyer of the miseries of birth and death, who seated on the ground under the banyan tree, grants knowledge to all the ṛṣis, who have assembled near Him.(2)
contentBlocks.meanings: वटविटपिसमीपे (vaṭaviṭapisamīpe) – near the banyan tree (vaṭa:banyan | viṭapi:tree | samīpe:near); भूमिभागे (bhūmibhāge) – on the ground (bhūmi:ground | bhāge:on); निषण्णं (niṣaṇṇaṃ) – seated; सकलमुनिजनानां (sakalamunijanānāṃ) – of all sages (sakala:all | muni:sage | janānāṃ:of people); ज्ञानदातारम् (jñānadātāram) – giver of knowledge (jñāna:knowledge | dātāram:giver); आरात् (ārāt) – from near; त्रिभुवनगुरुमीशं (tribhuvanagurumīśaṃ) – teacher of three worlds (tri:three | bhuvana:worlds | guru:teacher | īśaṃ:lord); दक्षिणामूर्तिदेवं (dakṣiṇāmūrtidevaṃ) – Dakshinamurti deity (dakṣiṇa:south | mūrti:form | devaṃ:deity); जननमरणदुःखच्छेद (jananamaraṇaduḥkhaccheda) – destroyer of birth-death sorrow (janana:birth | maraṇa:death | duḥkha:sorrow | ccheda:destroyer); दक्षं (dakṣaṃ) – capable; नमामि (namāmi) – I bow down;

contentBlocks.original:
चित्रं वटतरोर्मूले वृद्धाः शिष्याः गुरुर्युवा |
गुरोस्तु मौनव्याख्यानं शिष्यास्तुच्छिन्नसंशयाः ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
citraṃ vaṭatarormūle vṛddhāḥ śiṣyāḥ gururyuvā |
gurostu maunavyākhyānaṃ śiṣyās tu chinna saṃśayāḥ ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Wonderous indeed! Under the tree are the aged disciples around the youthful Guru. He taught them with silence, but the doubts of the disciples were all dispelled. (3)
contentBlocks.meanings: चित्रं (citraṃ) – wonderous; वटतरोः (vaṭataroḥ) – of the banyan tree (vaṭa:banyan | taroḥ:of the tree); मूले (mūle) – at the base; वृद्धाः (vṛddhāḥ) – aged; शिष्याः (śiṣyāḥ) – disciples; गुरुः (guruḥ) – Guru; युवा (yuvā) – youthful; गुरोः (guroḥ) – of the Guru; तु (tu) – but; मौनव्याख्यानं (maunavyākhyānaṃ) – silent teaching (mauna:silence | vyākhyānaṃ:teaching); शिष्याः (śiṣyāḥ) – disciples; तु (tu) – but; छिन्नसंशयाः (chinnasaṃśayāḥ) – doubts dispelled (chinna:cut off | saṃśayāḥ:doubts);

contentBlocks.original:
निधये सर्वविद्यानां भिषजे भवरोगिणाम् |
गुरवे सर्वलोकानां दक्षिणामूर्तये नमः ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nidhaye sarvavidyānāṃ bhiṣaje bhavarogiṇām |
gurave sarvalokānāṃ dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Dakshinamurti, the abode of all learning, the healer of all those who suffer from the disease of saṃsāra, and the teacher of the whole world.
contentBlocks.meanings: निधये (nidhaye) – abode; सर्वविद्यानाम् (sarvavidyānām) – of all learnings (sarva:all | vidyānām:of learnings); भिषजे (bhiṣaje) – to the healer; भवरोगिणाम् (bhavarogiṇām) – of those suffering from saṃsāra (bhava:saṃsāra | rogiṇām:of those suffering); गुरवे (gurave) – to the teacher; सर्वलोकानाम् (sarvalokānām) – of all worlds (sarva:all | lokānām:of worlds); दक्षिणामूर्तये (dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Dakshinamurti (dakṣiṇa:south | mūrtaye:to the form); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ नमः प्रणवार्थाय शुद्धज्ञानैकमूर्तये |
निर्मलाय प्रशान्ताय दक्षिणामूर्तये नमः ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ namaḥ praṇavārthāya śuddhajñānaikamūrtaye |
nirmalāya praśāntāya dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Dakshinamurti, who is ever pure and ever calm, the embodiment of pure knowledge and who is but the indicative meaning of Praṇava (Om) the Supreme. (5)
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – Om; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; प्रणवार्थाय (praṇavārthāya) – to the meaning of Om (praṇava:Om | arthāya:to the meaning); शुद्धज्ञानैकमूर्तये (śuddhajñānaikamūrtaye) – embodiment of pure knowledge (śuddha:pure | jñāna:knowledge | eka:one | mūrtaye:to the embodiment); निर्मलाय (nirmalāya) – to the pure; प्रशान्ताय (praśāntāya) – to the calm; दक्षिणामूर्तये (dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Dakshinamurti (dakṣiṇa:south | mūrti:form); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
चिदोघनाय महेशाय वटमूलनिवासिने |
सच्चिदानन्द रूपाय दक्षिणामूर्तये नमः ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
cidoghanāya maheśāya vaṭamūlanivāsine |
saccidānanda rūpāya dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Dakshinamurti Who is (as if) Consciousness Solidified, Salutations to the Mahesha (the Great God), Salutations to the One Who dwells at the root (i.e. base) of the Banyan Tree (Vata). Salutations to the embodiment of SatChitAnanda (Existence, Consciousness, Bliss) (6)
contentBlocks.meanings: चिदोघनाय (cidoghanāya) – to the solid consciousness (cit:consciousness | ghana:solid | āya:to); महेशाय (maheśāya) – to the great god (mahā:great | īśa:god | āya:to); वटमूलनिवासिने (vaṭamūlanivāsine) – to the one dwelling at the root of the banyan tree (vaṭa:banyan tree | mūla:root | nivāsine:to the one dwelling); सच्चिदानन्द (saccidānanda) – existence, consciousness, bliss (sat:existence | cit:consciousness | ānanda:bliss); रूपाय (rūpāya) – to the form; दक्षिणामूर्तये (dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Dakshinamurti; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
ईश्वरो गुरुरात्मेति मूर्तिभेदविभागिने |
व्योमवद् व्याप्तदेहाय दक्षिणामूर्तये नमः ‖
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśvaro gururātmeti mūrtibheda vibhāgine |
vyomavad vyāptadehāya dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ ‖
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Lord Dakshinamurti, who is all-pervasive like space but who appears (as though) divided as Lord, Guru, and the Self.
contentBlocks.meanings: ईश्वरः (īśvaraḥ) – Lord; गुरुः (guruḥ) – Guru; आत्मा (ātmā) – Self; इति (iti) – thus; मूर्तिभेदविभागिने (mūrtibhedavibhāgine) – to the one who appears divided (mūrti:form | bheda:division | vibhāgine:to the one who appears); व्योमवत् (vyomavat) – like space; व्याप्तदेहाय (vyāptadehāya) – to the all-pervasive one (vyāpta:pervasive | dehāya:to the one with a body); दक्षिणामूर्तये (dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Dakshinamurti; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
Stotram
विश्वं दर्पणदृश्यमाननगरी तुल्यं निजान्तर्गतं
पश्यन्नात्मनि मायया बहिरिवोद्भूतं यथा निद्रया।
यः साक्षात्कुरुते प्रबोधसमये स्वात्मानमेवाद्वयं
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvaṃ darpaṇadṛśyamānanagarītulyaṃ nijāntargataṃ
paśyannātmani māyayā bahirivodbhūtaṃ yathā nidrayā.
yaḥ sākṣātkurute prabodhasamaye svātmānamevādvayaṃ
tasmai śrīgurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 1..
contentBlocks.translation: He who experiences at the time of realization his own immutable Self-in which the Self alone plays as the universe of names and forms, like a city seen in a mirror, due to the maya power as though produced outside, as in a dream, to him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurti, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वं (viśvaṃ) – universe; दर्पणदृश्यमाननगरी (darpaṇadṛśyamānanagarī) – like a city seen in a mirror (darpaṇa:mirror | dṛśyamāna:seen | nagarī:city); तुल्यं (tulyaṃ) – like; निजान्तर्गतं (nijāntargataṃ) – contained within oneself (nija:own | antargataṃ:contained within); पश्यन् (paśyan) – seeing; आत्मनि (ātmani) – in the self; मायया (māyayā) – by illusion; बहिरिव (bahiriva) – as though outside (bahis:outside | iva:as though); उद्भूतं (udbhūtaṃ) – produced; यथा (yathā) – as; निद्रया (nidrayā) – by sleep; यः (yaḥ) – he who; साक्षात्कुरुते (sākṣātkurute) – realizes; प्रबोधसमये (prabodhasamaye) – at the time of realization (prabodha:realization | samaye:at the time); स्वात्मानमेव (svātmānameva) – his own self alone (sva:own | ātmānam:self | eva:alone); अद्वयं (advayaṃ) – non-dual; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrtaye:to the form); नम (nama) – prostration; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurti (śrī:Sri | dakṣiṇa:southern | mūrtaye:to the form);

contentBlocks.original:
बीजस्यान्तरिवाङ्कुरो जगदिदं प्रानिर्विकल्पं पुनः
मायाकल्पितदेशकालकलनावैचित्र्यचित्रीकृतम् ।
मायावीव विजृम्भयत्यपि महायोगीव यः स्वेच्छया
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bījasyāntarivāṅkuro jagadidaṃ prānirvikalpaṃ punaḥ
māyākalpitadeśakālakalanāvaicitryacitrīkṛtam .
māyāvīva vijṛmbhayatyapi mahāyogīva yaḥ svecchayā
tasmai śrīgurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 2..
contentBlocks.translation: He who, like a juggler or a great yogi, unrolls this universe just out of His own free will - the universe, which before creation remained unmanifest like the future tree in a seed and has later on projected Himself out to be the world of endless variety, due to the delusory play of time and space, both the products of maya... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurti, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: बीजस्य (bījasya) – of the seed; अन्तर (antara) – inside; इव (iva) – like; अङ्कुरः (aṅkuraḥ) – sprout; जगत् (jagat) – world; इदं (idaṃ) – this; प्र (pra) – forth; निर्विकल्पं (nirvikalpaṃ) – undifferentiated; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; माया (māyā) – illusion; कल्पित (kalpita) – imagined; देश (deśa) – space; काल (kāla) – time; कलना (kalanā) – calculation; वैचित्र्य (vaicitrya) – variety; चित्रीकृतम् (citrīkṛtam) – made varied; मायावी (māyāvī) – illusionist; इव (iva) – like; विजृम्भयति (vijṛmbhayati) – manifests; अपि (api) – also; महायोगी (mahāyogī) – great yogi (mahā:great | yogī:yogi); इव (iva) – like; यः (yaḥ) – who; स्वेच्छया (svecchayā) – by own will (sva:own | icchayā:by will); तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrtaye:to the form); नम (nama) – salutation; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurti (śrī:Sri | dakṣiṇa:southern | mūrtaye:to the form);

contentBlocks.original:
यस्यैव स्फुरणं सदात्मकमसत्कल्पार्थकं भासते
साक्षात्तत्त्वमसीति वेदवचसा यो बोधयत्याश्रितान् ।
यत्साक्षात्करणाद्भवेन्न पुनरावृत्तिर्भवाम्भोनिधौ
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyaiva sphuraṇaṃ sadātmakam asat kalpārthakaṃ bhāsate
sākṣāt tat-tvam-asīti veda vacasā yo bodhayaty āśritān .
yat sākṣāt karaṇād bhaven na punar āvṛttir bhavām bho nidhau
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 3..
contentBlocks.translation: He whose manifestations- which are themselves nothing but the Reality- appear as the objects of the world; He, who imparts to those who have surrendered to Him, direct enlightenment, through the vedic commandment "That Thou Art", and after the direct experience of which there is no more any return to the "ocean" of worldly existence.... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurty, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्य (yasya) – whose; एव (eva) – indeed; स्फुरणं (sphuraṇam) – manifestation; सदात्मकम् (sadātmakam) – of the nature of reality (sat:reality | ātmakam:of the nature); असत्कल्पार्थकम् (asatkalpārthakam) – appearing as unreal objects (asat:unreal | kalpa:appearing as | arthakam:objects); भासते (bhāsate) – shines; साक्षात् (sākṣāt) – directly; तत्त्वमसि (tat-tvam-asi) – That Thou Art (tat:that | tvam:thou | asi:art); इति (iti) – thus; वेदवचसा (veda-vacasā) – by the Vedic commandment (veda:Vedic | vacasā:by the commandment); यः (yaḥ) – who; बोधयति (bodhayati) – enlightens; आश्रितान् (āśritān) – those who have surrendered; यत् (yat) – which; साक्षात्करणात् (sākṣātkaraṇāt) – by direct realization (sākṣāt:direct | karaṇāt:by realization); भवेत् (bhavet) – there is; न (na) – no; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; आवृत्तिः (āvṛttiḥ) – return; भवाम्भोनिधौ (bhavāmbhonidhau) – to the ocean of existence (bhava:existence | ambho:ocean | nidhau:to); तस्मै (tasmai) – to Him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrī-gurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrtaye:to); नम (nama) – prostration; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrī-dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurty (śrī:Sri | dakṣiṇa:Dakshina | mūrtaye:to);

contentBlocks.original:
नानाच्छिद्र घटोदरस्थितमहा दीपप्रभाभास्वरं
ज्ञानं यस्य तु चक्षुरादिकरणद्वारा बहिः स्पन्दते ।
जानामीति तमेव भान्तमनुभात्येतत्समस्तं जगत्
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nānācchidra ghaṭodara sthitamahā dīpaprabhā bhāsvaraṃ
jñānaṃ yasya tu cakṣur ādi karaṇa dvārā bahiḥ spandate .
jānāmīti tameva bhāntam anubhāty etat samastaṃ jagat
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 4..
contentBlocks.translation: He, whose intelligence "flashes" outside through the eyes and other sense-organs, just like the bright light of a great lamp placed in a jar having many holes, and after whose shining, this whole universe of "objects" shines... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurthy, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: नानाच्छिद्र (nānācchidra) – many holes (nānā:many | chidra:holes); घटोदर (ghaṭodara) – inside a pot (ghaṭa:pot | udara:inside); स्थित (sthita) – situated; महा (mahā) – great; दीपप्रभा (dīpaprabhā) – lamp's light (dīpa:lamp | prabhā:light); भास्वरं (bhāsvaraṃ) – shining; ज्ञानं (jñānaṃ) – knowledge; यस्य (yasya) – whose; तु (tu) – but; चक्षुरादिकरणद्वारा (cakṣurādikaraṇadvārā) – through eyes and other senses (cakṣus:eyes | ādi:and others | karaṇa:senses | dvārā:through); बहिः (bahiḥ) – outside; स्पन्दते (spandate) – vibrates; जानामि (jānāmi) – I know; इति (iti) – thus; तम् (tam) – him; एव (eva) – indeed; भान्तम् (bhāntam) – shining; अनुभाति (anubhāti) – shines after; एतत् (etat) – this; समस्तं (samastaṃ) – entire; जगत् (jagat) – world; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrti:form | ye:to); नम (nama) – salutation; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurthy (śrī:Sri | dakṣiṇa:southern | mūrti:form | ye:to);

contentBlocks.original:
देहं प्राणमपीन्द्रियाण्यपि चलां बुद्धिं च शून्यं विदुः
स्त्रीबालान्धजडोपमास्त्वहमिति भ्रान्ता भृशं वादिनः ।
मायाशक्ति विलास कल्पितमहा व्यामोहसंहारिणे
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dehaṃ prāṇam api-indriyāṇy api calāṃ buddhiṃ ca śūnyaṃ viduḥ
strī bāla-andha jaḍo pamāstvaham iti bhrāntā bhṛśaṃ vādinaḥ .
māyā śaktivilāsa kalpitamahā vyāmoha saṃhāriṇe
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 5..
contentBlocks.translation: He who removes all the terrible misconceptions, created by the deluding play of maya- in those, who, intellectually innocent as a woman-child-blind-idiot and consider the reality as their body or their prana or their senses or their ever-changing intellect or as mere void and through error (mis-conceptions)- declare them to be the only reality... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurthy, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: देहं (dehaṃ) – body; प्राणम् (prāṇam) – life force; अपि (api) – also; इन्द्रियाणि (indriyāṇi) – senses; अपि (api) – also; चलां (calāṃ) – changing; बुद्धिं (buddhiṃ) – intellect; च (ca) – and; शून्यं (śūnyaṃ) – void; विदुः (viduḥ) – consider; स्त्रीबालान्धजडोपमाः (strībālāndhajaḍopamāḥ) – like women, children, blind, dull (strī:woman | bāla:child | andha:blind | jaḍa:dull | upamāḥ:like); तु (tu) – but; अहम् (aham) – I; इति (iti) – thus; भ्रान्ताः (bhrāntāḥ) – deluded; भृशं (bhṛśam) – greatly; वादिनः (vādinaḥ) – speakers; मायाशक्ति (māyāśakti) – power of illusion (māyā:illusion | śakti:power); विलास (vilāsa) – play; कल्पित (kalpita) – created; महा (mahā) – great; व्यामोह (vyāmoha) – delusion; संहारिणे (saṃhāriṇe) – remover; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrti:form); नम (nama) – salutation; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurthy (śrī:divine | dakṣiṇa:south | mūrti:form);

contentBlocks.original:
राहुग्रस्तदिवाकरेन्दुसदृशो मायासमाच्छादनात्
सन्मात्रः करणोपसंहरणतो योऽभूत्सुषुप्तः पुमान् ।
प्रागस्वाप्समिति प्रबोधसमये यः प्रत्यभिज्ञायते
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāhu grasta divākarendu sadṛśo māyā samācchādanāt
sanmātraḥ karaṇopasaṃharaṇato yo'bhūt suṣuptaḥ pumān .
prāgasvāpsamiti prabodhasamaye yaḥ pratyabhijñāyate
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrīd akṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 6..
contentBlocks.translation: On folding up all the functions of the senses, He who enters into a state of deep-sleep and there becomes existence veiled in maya, like the sun or the moon during eclipse, and who, on waking, remembers to have slept... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurthy, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: राहुग्रस्तदिवाकरेन्दुसदृशः (rāhugrastadivākarendusadṛśaḥ) – like the sun and moon eclipsed (rāhu:Rahu | grasta:eclipsed | divākara:sun | indu:moon | sadṛśaḥ:like); मायासमाच्छादनात् (māyāsamācchādanāt) – due to maya's covering (māyā:maya | samācchādanāt:due to covering); सन्मात्रः (sanmātraḥ) – pure existence (sat:existence | mātraḥ:only); करणोपसंहरणतः (karaṇopasaṃharaṇataḥ) – by withdrawing the senses (karaṇa:senses | upasaṃharaṇataḥ:by withdrawing); यः (yaḥ) – who; अभूत् (abhūt) – became; सुषुप्तः (suṣuptaḥ) – deep sleep; पुमान् (pumān) – man; प्राक् (prāk) – before; अस्वाप्सम् (asvāpsam) – I slept; इति (iti) – thus; प्रबोधसमये (prabodhasamaye) – at the time of awakening (prabodha:awakening | samaye:at the time); यः (yaḥ) – who; प्रत्यभिज्ञायते (pratyabhijñāyate) – is recognized; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrtaye:to the form); नम (nama) – salutation; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurthy (śrī:Sri | dakṣiṇa:south | mūrtaye:to the form);

contentBlocks.original:
बाल्यादिष्वपि जाग्रदादिषु तथा सर्वास्ववस्थास्वपि
व्यावृत्तास्वनुवर्तमानमहमित्यन्तः स्फुरन्तं सदा ।
स्वात्मानं प्रकटीकरोति भजतां यो मुद्रया भद्रया
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bālyādiṣvapi jāgradādiṣu tathā sarvāsvavasthāsvapi
vyāvṛttāsv anuvartamānam aham ity antaḥ sphurantaṃ sadā .
svātmānaṃ prakaṭīkaroti bhajatāṃ yo mudrayā bhadrayā
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 7..
contentBlocks.translation: He, who, through the auspicious sign of knowledge (jnana-mudra), reveals to his devotees Him own Self- which persists in all stages of age (childhood, boyhood, youth and old age), in all states (waking, dreaming and deep-sleep) and in all other conditions- and who constantly manifests Himself inwardly as "I"... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurthy, is the prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: बाल्यादिषु (bālyādiṣu) – in childhood and others (bālya:childhood | ādiṣu:and others); अपि (api) – even; जाग्रदादिषु (jāgradādiṣu) – in waking and others (jāgrat:waking | ādiṣu:and others); तथा (tathā) – similarly; सर्वास्ववस्थास्वपि (sarvāsvavasthāsvapi) – in all states even (sarvāsu:in all | avasthāsu:states | api:even); व्यावृत्तासु (vyāvṛttāsu) – in the distinct; अनुवर्तमानम् (anuvartamānam) – continuing; अहम् (aham) – I; इति (iti) – thus; अन्तः (antaḥ) – within; स्फुरन्तम् (sphurantaṃ) – shining; सदा (sadā) – always; स्वात्मानम् (svātmānam) – own Self; प्रकटीकरोति (prakaṭīkaroti) – reveals; भजताम् (bhajatām) – to the devotees; यः (yaḥ) – who; मुद्रया (mudrayā) – by the sign; भद्रया (bhadrayā) – auspicious; तस्मै (tasmai) – to Him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrtaye:to the form); नम (nama) – prostration; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurthy (śrī:divine | dakṣiṇa:south | mūrtaye:to the form);

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वं पश्यति कार्यकारणतया स्वस्वामिसंबन्धतः
शिष्याचार्यतया तथैव पितृपुत्राद्यात्मना भेदतः ।
स्वप्ने जाग्रति वा य एष पुरुषो मायापरिभ्रामितः
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvaṃ paśyati kārya kāraṇatayā sva svāmi saṃbandhataḥ
śiṣyācāryatayā tathaiva pitṛputrādyātmanā bhedataḥ .
svapne jāgrati vā ya eṣa puruṣo māyāparibhrāmitaḥ
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 8..
contentBlocks.translation: He, who is the Purusa, whirled in maya, sees, in Himself, the world of cause-effect diversely related as possessor and possession, father and son, and as teacher and taught, both in the state of waking and of dreaming;... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurthy, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वं (viśvam) – the world; पश्यति (paśyati) – sees; कार्यकारणतया (kāryakāraṇatayā) – as cause and effect (kārya:effect | kāraṇa:cause | tayā:as); स्वस्वामिसंबन्धतः (svasvāmi-saṃbandhataḥ) – as possessor and possession (sva:own | svāmi:master | saṃbandhataḥ:in relation); शिष्याचार्यतया (śiṣyācāryatayā) – as teacher and taught (śiṣya:student | ācārya:teacher | tayā:as); तथैव (tathaiva) – and also; पितृपुत्राद्यात्मना (pitṛputrādyātmanā) – as father and son, etc. (pitṛ:father | putra:son | ādi:etc. | ātmanā:as); भेदतः (bhedataḥ) – in diversity; स्वप्ने (svapne) – in dream; जाग्रति (jāgrati) – in waking; वा (vā) – or; य (ya) – who; एष (eṣa) – this; पुरुषः (puruṣaḥ) – person; मायापरिभ्रामितः (māyāparibhrāmitaḥ) – deluded by illusion (māyā:illusion | paribhrāmitaḥ:deluded); तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrtaye:to the form); नम (nama) – salutation; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurthy (śrī:Sri | dakṣiṇa:south | mūrtaye:to the form);

contentBlocks.original:
भूरम्भां स्यनलोऽनिलोऽम्बरमहर्नाथो हिमांशुः पुमान्
इत्याभाति चराचरात्मकमिदं यस्यैव मूर्त्यष्टकम् ।
नान्यत्किञ्चन विद्यते विमृशतां यस्मात्परस्माद्विभोः
तस्मै श्रीगुरुमूर्तये नम इदं श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये ॥ ९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūr ambhāṃsy analo'nilo'mbaram aharnātho himāṃśuḥ pumān
ityābhāti carācara ātmakamidaṃ yasyaiva mūrtyaṣṭakam .
nānyat kiñcana vidyate vimṛśatāṃ yasmāt parasmād vibhoḥ
tasmai śrī gurumūrtaye nama idaṃ śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye .. 9..
contentBlocks.translation: He, whose eight-fold forms are the "Earth, Water, Fire, Air, Ether, Sun, Moon and Jiva", and who manifests Himself as this universe of the movable and immovable objects- and besides which, the Supreme all-pervading lord, there exists nothing to those who reflect well upon... to Him, the divine teacher, Sri Dakshinamurthy, is this prostration.
contentBlocks.meanings: भूः (bhūḥ) – Earth; अम्भांसि (ambhāṃsi) – Water; अनलः (analaḥ) – Fire; अनिलः (anilaḥ) – Air; अम्बरम् (ambaram) – Ether; अहर्नाथः (aharnāthaḥ) – Sun; हिमांशुः (himāṃśuḥ) – Moon; पुमान् (pumān) – Jiva; इति (iti) – thus; आभाति (ābhāti) – manifests; चराचरात्मकम् (carācarātmakam) – of movable and immovable (cara:movable | acara:immovable | ātmakam:nature of); इदं (idaṃ) – this; यस्मै (yasmai) – to whom; एव (eva) – indeed; मूर्त्यष्टकम् (mūrtyaṣṭakam) – eight-fold forms (mūrti:form | aṣṭakam:eight-fold); न (na) – not; अन्यत् (anyat) – other; किञ्चन (kiñcana) – anything; विद्यते (vidyate) – exists; विमृशताम् (vimṛśatām) – to those who reflect; यस्मात् (yasmāt) – from which; परस्मात् (parasmāt) – supreme; विभोः (vibhoḥ) – all-pervading lord; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; श्रीगुरुमूर्तये (śrīgurumūrtaye) – to the divine teacher (śrī:divine | guru:teacher | mūrti:form); नम (nama) – prostration; इदं (idaṃ) – this; श्रीदक्षिणामूर्तये (śrīdakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Sri Dakshinamurthy (śrī:divine | dakṣiṇa:south | mūrti:form);

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वात्मत्वमिति स्फुटीकृतमिदं यस्मादमुष्मिन् स्तवे
तेनास्य श्रवणात्तदर्थमननाद्ध्यानाच्च सङ्कीर्तनात् ।
सर्वात्मत्वमहाविभूतिसहितं स्यादीश्वरत्वं स्वतः
सिद्ध्येत्तत्पुनरष्टधा परिणतं चैश्वर्यमव्याहतम् ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvātmatvam iti sphuṭīkṛtam idaṃ yasmād amuṣmin stave
tenāsya śravaṇāt tadartha mananād dhyānād ca saṅkīrtanāt .
sarvātmatva mahā vibhūti sahitaṃ syād īśvaratvaṃ svataḥ
siddhyet tat punar aṣṭadhā pariṇataṃ ca iśvaryam avyāhatam .. 10..
contentBlocks.translation: The Knowledge "all-this-ātman" (Sarva-ātmatvam) has been explained in this hymn and so, by hearing it, by reflecting and meditating upon its meaning and by reciting it, one will attain that divine state, endued with the glory of the all-Self-hood, along with the permanent eight-fold holy, powers of Godhood.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वात्मत्वम् (sarvātmatvam) – all-Self-hood (sarva:all | ātmatvam:Self-hood); इति (iti) – thus; स्फुटीकृतम् (sphuṭīkṛtam) – explained; इदं (idaṃ) – this; यस्मात् (yasmād) – because; अमुष्मिन् (amuṣmin) – in this; स्तवे (stave) – hymn; तेन (tena) – by that; अस्य (asya) – of this; श्रवणात् (śravaṇāt) – by hearing; तदर्थ (tadartha) – its meaning (tad:its | artha:meaning); मननात् (mananād) – by reflecting; ध्यानात् (dhyānād) – by meditating; च (ca) – and; सङ्कीर्तनात् (saṅkīrtanāt) – by reciting; सर्वात्मत्वमहाविभूतिसहितम् (sarvātmatva-mahā-vibhūti-sahitam) – endued with great glory (sarvātmatva:all-Self-hood | mahā:great | vibhūti:glory | sahitam:endued with); स्यात् (syāt) – would be; ईश्वरत्वं (īśvaratvaṃ) – Godhood; स्वतः (svataḥ) – by oneself; सिद्ध्येत् (siddhyet) – attain; तत् (tat) – that; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; अष्टधा (aṣṭadhā) – eight-fold; परिणतम् (pariṇatam) – transformed; च (ca) – and; ऐश्वर्यम् (aiśvaryam) – powers of Godhood; अव्याहतम् (avyāhatam) – unobstructed;
